title: magnolia × kewensis
intro: Magnolia × kewensis, also known as the Kew Magnolia, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Magnolia denudata and Magnolia liliiflora, known for its stunning flowers and compact growth habit. The bark and roots of the Magnolia × kewensis plant are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, due to their reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These medicinal properties are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for its medicinal use, as well as being used in aromatherapy for its calming and relaxing effects.
